name: React Native — list performance (HIGH)
description: Virtualize any scrollable list (LegendList / FlashList / FlatList); avoid inline objects/styles in `renderItem`; hoist stable callbacks; keep rows lightweight (no per-row queries); stable `data` references; pass primitives for `memo`; compressed thumbnails; `getItemType` for heterogeneous lists.

metadata.intent: Optimize virtualized lists for scroll performance and predictable memoization

must_use: Stable references for `data` item objects where possible
must_not: Map/filter to new object array on every keystroke for list `data` when virtualization depends on stability
conventions: Move fetching & heavy compute to parent; rows are thin render functions.

steps.description: Replace `ScrollView` + `.map` for non-trivial lists with LegendList/FlashList/FlatList + sensible `estimatedItemSize` / keys.
Avoid new object literals in `renderItem` (props/style). Pass `item` or primitives; hoist static style objects to module scope.
Pass stable press handlers (root `useCallback` + id param, or handler inside memoized row keyed by id) — avoid fresh inline `() =>` per row when it breaks memoization.
Remove `useQuery`/heavy hooks from row components; prefer store selectors with narrow subscriptions vs broad context.
When using `memo`, prefer primitive props so shallow compare matches user intent.
Use CDN resize / ~2x display size thumbnails in lists; prefer optimized image components (see `react-native-ui-patterns`).
For mixed row types, tag items with `type` and use `getItemType` (+ per-type size estimates) so recycling pools stay correct.
